roadmap.target_role: Software Engineer at a Product-Based Company
roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 6 milestones: An intermediate web developer with 2 years of experience aiming for a Software Engineer role at a product-based company requires a significant transformation. This involves mastering Advanced DSA, System Design (both High-Level and Low-Level), and comprehensive interview problem-solving. This wide capability gap, combined with the need for distinct identity transitions from a foundational problem solver to an interview-ready architect, necessitates 6 focused milestones to ensure adequate depth and progression, despite the tight time budget which will extend the overall duration.

vision_profile.current_state: A Web Developer with Python/Django and basic DSA, feeling stuck and fearing difficult product-based company interviews.
vision_profile.main_blocker: The fear of not being able to crack difficult interviews for product-based companies due to gaps in Advanced DSA, System Design, and overall interview problem-solving skills.
vision_profile.top_motivation: To switch to a Product-Based Company as a Software Engineer for better career growth and to overcome current career stagnation.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: The learner can confidently solve medium-difficulty algorithmic problems using advanced data structures and fundamental algorithmic paradigms, demonstrating a solid base for product company coding rounds.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules: This milestone focuses on building a strong foundation in advanced data structures and core algorithmic techniques, requiring distinct capability clusters for data structure mastery, initial algorithmic patterns, and problem-solving strategies.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a meticulous problem solver, building the robust algorithmic foundation essential for any top-tier software engineering role.

project.title: Optimized Data Processing Engine
project.description: Design and implement a Python-based data processing engine that efficiently handles large datasets using advanced data structures (e.g., custom hash maps, balanced trees) and optimized algorithms. The engine should process incoming data streams, perform aggregations, and store results for quick retrieval.
project.deliverable: A functional Python application with unit tests, clear documentation, and a performance report demonstrating efficiency improvements over naive approaches.
seeded_errors: The AI-generated initial data structure choice leads to O(N^2) complexity for a critical operation, requiring the learner to identify and replace it with an O(N log N) or O(N) alternative.

modules.title: Advanced Data Structures
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills: This module covers the essential advanced data structures beyond basic arrays and lists, which are critical for efficient problem-solving in product companies.

skills.title: Trees and Graphs
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering trees and graphs is fundamental for modeling complex relationships and optimizing search/traversal problems, frequently encountered in system design and algorithmic challenges.

scenario.title: Optimizing Network Routing with Graphs

skills.title: Heaps and Priority Queues
skills.why_this_skill: Heaps are crucial for efficiently managing priorities and finding extreme values, vital for algorithms like Dijkstra's and in real-time scheduling systems.

scenario.title: Real-time Task Scheduling with Priority Queues

skills.title: Advanced Hash Tables
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding advanced hashing techniques and collision resolution is vital for building efficient data storage and retrieval systems, a cornerstone of many software applications.

scenario.title: Debugging Hash Collisions in a Caching System

skills.title: Disjoint Set Union (DSU)
skills.why_this_skill: DSU is a powerful data structure for efficiently tracking connected components, essential for problems involving network connectivity, clustering, and graph algorithms.
lessons: DSU with Path Compression & Union by Rank

scenario.title: Detecting Cycles in a Social Network Graph

modules.title: Algorithmic Paradigms I
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module introduces fundamental algorithmic design paradigms that are crucial for breaking down and solving complex problems systematically.

skills.title: Recursion and Backtracking
skills.why_this_skill: Recursion is a powerful problem-solving technique, and backtracking is essential for exploring all possible solutions in combinatorial problems, common in interviews.

scenario.title: Optimizing a Maze Solver with Backtracking

skills.title: Divide and Conquer
skills.why_this_skill: This paradigm is key for breaking down large problems into smaller, manageable sub-problems, leading to efficient solutions for sorting, searching, and geometric problems.

scenario.title: Parallelizing Large Data Sorting

skills.title: Greedy Algorithms
skills.why_this_skill: Greedy approaches offer simple yet effective solutions for optimization problems where local optimal choices lead to a global optimum, such as scheduling or coin change problems.
lessons: Proof of Optimality for Greedy Choices

scenario.title: Resource Allocation in a Cloud Environment

modules.title: Problem Solving Strategies
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module focuses on practical techniques for approaching and solving algorithmic problems, including complexity analysis and common patterns.

skills.title: Time and Space Complexity Analysis
skills.why_this_skill: A critical skill for evaluating algorithm efficiency and making informed design choices, directly impacting system performance and scalability.

scenario.title: Identifying Performance Bottlenecks in Production Code

skills.title: Two Pointers and Sliding Window
skills.why_this_skill: These patterns are highly effective for optimizing array and string manipulation problems, reducing time complexity from quadratic to linear in many cases.

video.title: Mastering Two Pointers and Sliding Window

scenario.title: Finding Subarrays with Specific Properties

skills.title: Prefix Sums and Difference Arrays
skills.why_this_skill: These techniques enable constant-time range queries and updates, significantly optimizing problems involving array segments and intervals.

video.title: Efficient Range Queries with Prefix Sums

scenario.title: Analyzing Financial Time Series Data

science.desc: Design and implement a system to find the k-th largest element in a continuous stream of numbers. Discuss time and space complexity.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: The learner can design and implement efficient solutions for complex algorithmic problems, including dynamic programming and graph algorithms, demonstrating strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules: This milestone deepens algorithmic expertise, focusing on advanced paradigms like dynamic programming and graph theory, which require dedicated modules for conceptual understanding and application.
milestones.identity_statement: You are evolving into an algorithmic architect, capable of constructing elegant and efficient solutions to the most challenging computational puzzles.

project.title: Route Optimization Service
project.description: Develop a service that finds optimal routes in a weighted graph, considering various constraints like shortest path, fastest path, or paths avoiding certain nodes. Implement algorithms like Dijkstra's and potentially Bellman-Ford or Floyd-Warshall.
project.deliverable: A Python service with a clear API, graph data loading, route calculation, and visualization of paths. Include unit tests and performance benchmarks.
seeded_errors: The AI-generated graph representation or algorithm implementation fails to correctly handle negative edge weights, leading to incorrect shortest path calculations in specific scenarios.

modules.title: Dynamic Programming
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module covers the core concepts and advanced applications of dynamic programming, a critical technique for solving optimization problems.

skills.title: Memoization and Tabulation
skills.why_this_skill: These are the two primary approaches to dynamic programming, essential for optimizing recursive solutions by avoiding redundant computations.

scenario.title: Optimizing a Financial Portfolio with DP

skills.title: Knapsack Problem Variations
skills.why_this_skill: The Knapsack problem is a classic DP problem with numerous real-world applications in resource allocation, inventory management, and project selection.

scenario.title: Optimizing Server Rack Space Allocation

skills.title: Longest Common Subsequence (LCS)
skills.why_this_skill: LCS is a foundational DP problem with applications in bioinformatics, file comparison (diff tools), and version control systems.

scenario.title: Implementing a Document Version Control Feature

modules.title: Graph Algorithms
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills: This module covers essential graph traversal and shortest path algorithms, which are frequently tested and applied in real-world systems.

skills.title: Breadth-First Search (BFS) and Depth-First Search (DFS)
skills.why_this_skill: BFS and DFS are fundamental graph traversal algorithms, forming the basis for many other graph-related problems and system explorations.

video.title: BFS and DFS for Graph Traversal

scenario.title: Finding Connected Components in a Social Network

skills.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm
skills.why_this_skill: Dijkstra's algorithm is the standard for finding the shortest paths in graphs with non-negative edge weights, crucial for navigation and network routing.

scenario.title: Optimizing Delivery Routes for a Logistics Company

skills.title: Minimum Spanning Trees (MST)
skills.why_this_skill: MST algorithms (Prim's, Kruskal's) are vital for designing efficient networks, minimizing connection costs, and solving clustering problems.

scenario.title: Designing an Efficient Telecommunication Network

skills.title: Topological Sort
skills.why_this_skill: Topological sorting is essential for scheduling tasks with dependencies, such as build systems, course prerequisites, or job pipelines.
lessons: Kahn's Algorithm and DFS-based Topological Sort

scenario.title: Scheduling Dependencies in a CI/CD Pipeline

modules.title: Advanced Search & Sorting
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module refines search and sorting techniques, focusing on advanced variations and their applications in complex data scenarios.

skills.title: Binary Search Variations
skills.why_this_skill: Beyond basic binary search, understanding its variations is crucial for solving problems on sorted arrays, finding boundaries, and optimizing search spaces.

scenario.title: Optimizing Database Index Lookups

skills.title: Sorting & Searching
skills.why_this_skill: These non-comparison sorts offer linear time complexity for specific data distributions, providing highly efficient alternatives to general-purpose sorting algorithms.

scenario.title: Sorting Large Datasets of Integers Efficiently

skills.title: Ternary Search
skills.why_this_skill: Ternary search is an optimization technique for finding the minimum or maximum of a unimodal function, useful in competitive programming and specific optimization problems.

scenario.title: Optimizing a Convex Function for Resource Allocation

science.desc: Given a list of tasks with dependencies, find a valid order to perform the tasks. Discuss how to handle cyclic dependencies.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: The learner can articulate the fundamental components and trade-offs of distributed systems, design basic system architectures, and discuss key considerations for scalability and reliability.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules: This milestone introduces the foundational concepts of High-Level System Design, requiring distinct modules for core principles, networking, and data storage to build a comprehensive understanding.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a core system designer, understanding the building blocks of large-scale applications and how they interact to deliver robust services.

project.title: Distributed URL Shortener
project.description: Design and architect a highly available and scalable URL shortening service. Focus on key components like ID generation, storage, redirection, and handling high traffic. Document the system's architecture, API design, and scaling strategies.
project.deliverable: A detailed system design document (HLD) including component diagrams, API specifications, data schema, and a discussion of trade-offs and scaling considerations.
seeded_errors: The AI-generated initial design for the ID generation service has a single point of failure and does not scale horizontally, requiring the learner to propose a distributed, fault-tolerant alternative.

modules.title: Foundations of System Design
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills: This module establishes the core principles and non-functional requirements that drive system design decisions, essential for any scalable application.

skills.title: Scalability & Performance
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding how to design systems that can handle increasing load and adapt to changing demands is paramount for product-based companies.

scenario.title: Handling a Sudden Spike in User Traffic

skills.title: Availability
skills.why_this_skill: Designing for high availability and fault tolerance is critical to ensure systems remain operational and perform consistently, minimizing downtime and data loss.

scenario.title: Designing for Disaster Recovery in a Multi-Region Setup

skills.title: Caching & Performance Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: Optimizing for low latency and high throughput is crucial for user experience and system efficiency, especially in real-time applications.

scenario.title: Diagnosing High Latency in a Microservice

skills.title: Model Evaluation
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding different consistency models (e.g., strong, eventual) is crucial for designing distributed data stores and ensuring data integrity in complex systems.

scenario.title: Resolving Data Inconsistencies in a Replicated Database

modules.title: Networking & Communication
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module covers the essential networking concepts and communication patterns required for building distributed systems.

skills.title: Neural Networks
skills.why_this_skill: A deep understanding of network protocols is foundational for designing reliable and efficient communication between services in a distributed environment.

scenario.title: Troubleshooting Network Latency Issues

skills.title: API Design & Service Layer
skills.why_this_skill: Load balancers are critical for distributing traffic, ensuring high availability, and improving the performance of web services and microservices.

scenario.title: Configuring a Load Balancer for High Traffic API

skills.title: API Design & Service Layer
skills.why_this_skill: API Gateways are crucial for managing external traffic, providing security, rate limiting, and routing to microservices, simplifying client-side interactions.

scenario.title: Implementing Authentication and Rate Limiting with an API Gateway

modules.title: Databases & Storage
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills: This module covers various database types, their trade-offs, and strategies for efficient data storage and retrieval in distributed systems.

skills.title: SQL vs NoSQL
skills.why_this_skill: Choosing the right database type is fundamental to system design, impacting scalability, data consistency, and development flexibility.

scenario.title: Choosing a Database for a Social Media Feed

skills.title: Caching & Performance Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: Effective caching is crucial for reducing database load, improving response times, and enhancing the scalability of read-heavy applications.

scenario.title: Implementing a Distributed Cache for a High-Traffic E-commerce Site

skills.title: Replication
skills.why_this_skill: Replication is essential for ensuring data availability, fault tolerance, and read scalability in production databases.

scenario.title: Setting Up Database Replication for High Availability

skills.title: Distributed Systems
skills.why_this_skill: Managing transactions across multiple services or databases is a complex but necessary aspect of building robust distributed systems.

scenario.title: Ensuring Atomicity in a Microservice Payment System

science.desc: Design a system to store and retrieve user session data for a large-scale web application. Discuss trade-offs between different storage solutions and caching strategies.

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: The learner can design scalable, performant, and reliable distributed systems, applying common architectural patterns like microservices and understanding advanced distributed system concepts.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules: This milestone builds upon foundational system design, delving into advanced distributed systems, microservices, and performance optimization, each requiring a dedicated focus.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a scalable solutions engineer, capable of architecting and optimizing complex distributed systems to meet high demands and ensure continuous operation.

project.title: Real-time Notification System
project.description: Architect a real-time notification system capable of sending millions of messages per second to users across various channels (web push, mobile push, email). Focus on message queues, pub/sub patterns, and fault tolerance.
project.deliverable: A comprehensive system design document detailing message flow, component interactions, scalability mechanisms (sharding, replication), and a plan for deployment and monitoring.
seeded_errors: The AI-suggested message queue configuration lacks proper dead-letter queue handling and retry mechanisms, leading to lost messages under transient failures. The learner must propose a more robust error handling strategy.

modules.title: Distributed Systems Concepts
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills: This module covers advanced concepts in distributed systems, including consensus, messaging, and distributed coordination, which are crucial for building robust and fault-tolerant architectures.

skills.title: Distributed Systems
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding consensus algorithms is vital for building highly available and consistent distributed systems, especially for critical services like leader election or distributed locks.

scenario.title: Designing a Fault-Tolerant Leader Election Service

skills.title: Message Queues
skills.why_this_skill: Asynchronous communication patterns like message queues and publish-subscribe are essential for decoupling services, improving responsiveness, and handling high message volumes.

scenario.title: Designing a High-Throughput Event Streaming Platform

skills.title: Distributed Systems
skills.why_this_skill: In complex microservices architectures, distributed tracing is indispensable for understanding request flows, debugging performance issues, and identifying bottlenecks across services.

scenario.title: Debugging a Latency Spike Across Multiple Microservices

skills.title: Event-Driven Architecture
skills.why_this_skill: Event-driven architectures enable highly scalable, decoupled, and responsive systems, crucial for modern, reactive applications.

scenario.title: Migrating a Monolithic E-commerce System to Event-Driven Microservices

modules.title: Microservices Architecture
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module focuses on the design, implementation, and operational aspects of microservices, a prevalent architectural style in product companies.

skills.title: Microservices Architecture
skills.why_this_skill: Properly decomposing a monolithic application into microservices is crucial for achieving scalability, maintainability, and independent deployment.

scenario.title: Defining Service Boundaries for a New Feature

skills.title: Service Communication Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Choosing appropriate communication patterns (e.g., REST, gRPC, message brokers) is vital for efficient and reliable interaction between microservices.

scenario.title: Choosing Communication Protocols for a New Microservice

skills.title: Service Discovery
skills.why_this_skill: Service discovery is essential for microservices to locate and communicate with each other dynamically, especially in highly elastic and distributed environments.

scenario.title: Integrating a New Service into an Existing Microservices Ecosystem

modules.title: Scalability & Performance
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module focuses on advanced techniques for scaling systems and optimizing their performance under heavy loads, including data partitioning and concurrency management.

skills.title: Sharding
skills.why_this_skill: Sharding is a critical technique for scaling databases horizontally, distributing data across multiple servers to handle massive datasets and high query volumes.

scenario.title: Designing a Sharding Strategy for a Global User Base

skills.title: Concurrency & Multithreading
skills.why_this_skill: Leveraging concurrency and parallelism is essential for maximizing resource utilization and improving the throughput of applications, especially in multi-core environments.

scenario.title: Optimizing a Batch Processing Job for Parallel Execution

skills.title: Monitoring & Observability
skills.why_this_skill: Proactive monitoring and alerting are essential for identifying performance degradation, detecting anomalies, and ensuring the health of production systems.

scenario.title: Responding to a Critical Performance Alert in Production

science.desc: Design a system to handle 10 million daily active users for a social media platform. Focus on the feed generation and delivery mechanism, discussing scalability and consistency.

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: The learner can design robust, maintainable, and extensible software components using object-oriented principles, apply common design patterns, and create well-defined APIs for services.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules: This milestone focuses on Low-Level System Design, covering object-oriented principles, design patterns, and API design, each forming a distinct capability for building high-quality software components.
milestones.identity_statement: You are transforming into an object-oriented architect, crafting elegant and maintainable codebases that form the backbone of complex systems.

project.title: Extensible Plugin Management System
project.description: Develop a core application with an extensible plugin architecture using OOP principles and design patterns (e.g., Strategy, Observer, Factory). The system should allow new functionalities to be added dynamically without modifying the core code.
project.deliverable: A Python application demonstrating the plugin system, including a core module and at least two example plugins, with unit tests and a design document explaining the chosen patterns.
seeded_errors: The AI-generated initial plugin loading mechanism introduces tight coupling between the core system and plugins, violating the Open/Closed Principle. The learner must refactor it to use a more flexible, decoupled approach.

modules.title: OOP & SOLID Principles
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills: This module covers the foundational principles of Object-Oriented Programming and the SOLID principles, which are crucial for writing maintainable, flexible, and scalable code.

skills.title: Object-Oriented Principles (Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, Abstraction)
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering OOP principles is fundamental for designing modular, reusable, and understandable codebases, which is a core expectation in product companies.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Monolithic Class into Modular Components

skills.title: Single Responsibility Principle (SRP)
skills.why_this_skill: SRP ensures that each class or module has only one reason to change, leading to more robust, testable, and maintainable code.

scenario.title: Refactoring a God Class in a Legacy System

skills.title: SOLID Principles
skills.why_this_skill: OCP promotes designing software entities that are open for extension but closed for modification, leading to flexible and maintainable systems that can evolve without breaking existing code.

scenario.title: Adding a New Payment Gateway to an E-commerce System

skills.title: Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP)
skills.why_this_skill: LSP ensures that derived classes can be substituted for their base classes without altering the correctness of the program, maintaining type safety and promoting robust inheritance hierarchies.

scenario.title: Identifying LSP Violations in a Shape Hierarchy

modules.title: Design Patterns
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills: This module introduces essential design patterns (Creational, Structural, Behavioral), providing reusable solutions to common software design problems.

skills.title: Behavioral Design Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Creational patterns provide flexible ways to create objects, decoupling the client from the concrete classes and promoting modularity.

scenario.title: Designing a Cross-Platform UI Component Factory

skills.title: Behavioral Design Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Structural patterns help compose classes and objects into larger structures, promoting flexibility and efficiency in system design.

scenario.title: Integrating a Legacy API with an Adapter Pattern

skills.title: Behavioral Design Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Behavioral patterns address how objects interact and distribute responsibilities, improving flexibility and communication within a system.

video.title: Managing Object Interactions with Behavioral Patterns

scenario.title: Implementing a Flexible Notification System with Observer Pattern

skills.title: Dependency Injection
skills.why_this_skill: Dependency Injection promotes loose coupling and testability by externalizing dependency creation, making code more flexible and easier to manage.

video.title: Achieving Loose Coupling with Dependency Injection

scenario.title: Refactoring a Service to Use Dependency Injection for Testability

modules.title: API Design & Service Layer
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module focuses on designing clean, consistent, and efficient APIs, which are the interface to any modern software service.

skills.title: API Design & Service Layer
skills.why_this_skill: Designing well-structured RESTful APIs is crucial for interoperability, ease of use, and maintainability of web services.

scenario.title: Designing a REST API for a New E-commerce Product Catalog

skills.title: API Design & Service Layer
skills.why_this_skill: Managing API changes and versions gracefully is crucial for maintaining backward compatibility and ensuring smooth transitions for clients.

scenario.title: Deprecating an Old API Version Without Breaking Clients

skills.title: API Design & Service Layer
skills.why_this_skill: GraphQL offers a flexible and efficient alternative to REST for data fetching, allowing clients to request exactly what they need, reducing over-fetching and under-fetching.

scenario.title: Designing a GraphQL API for a Mobile Application Backend

science.desc: Design the core components and API for a ride-sharing service. Focus on the low-level design of the 'matching engine' and how it interacts with other services.

milestones.label: M06
milestones.o: The learner is fully prepared to ace technical interviews for product-based companies, demonstrating mastery in Advanced DSA, High-Level System Design, Low-Level System Design, and effective communication of solutions.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules: This final milestone integrates all learned skills, focusing on advanced problem-solving, comprehensive system design case studies, and intensive interview preparation to ensure job readiness.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now an interview-ready software engineer, equipped with the knowledge, skills, and confidence to secure your dream role at a product-based company.

project.title: End-to-End Product Search Engine
project.description: Design and outline the implementation of a scalable product search engine for an e-commerce platform. This involves integrating advanced data structures for indexing, system design for distributed search, and low-level design for query processing and ranking.
project.deliverable: A comprehensive system design document (HLD & LLD) covering indexing, query processing, ranking, scalability, fault tolerance, and API design. Include pseudocode for critical algorithmic components.
seeded_errors: The AI-generated initial ranking algorithm is too simplistic and doesn't account for relevance factors beyond exact keyword matches. The learner must propose an improved ranking strategy incorporating factors like popularity, recency, and personalization.

modules.title: Advanced Problem Solving & Optimization
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills: This module focuses on highly specialized algorithmic techniques and mathematical foundations often encountered in advanced coding interviews.

skills.title: Bit Manipulation
skills.why_this_skill: Bit manipulation is crucial for optimizing solutions, especially in competitive programming and low-level system design, by performing operations directly on binary representations.

scenario.title: Implementing a Fast Set Data Structure

skills.title: Mathematics for Algorithms (Number Theory, Combinatorics)
skills.why_this_skill: A strong grasp of mathematical concepts like number theory and combinatorics is essential for solving advanced algorithmic problems and understanding cryptographic principles.

scenario.title: Calculating Combinations for a Lottery System

skills.title: String Algorithms (KMP, Rabin-Karp)
skills.why_this_skill: Efficient string algorithms are essential for text processing, pattern matching, and bioinformatics, frequently appearing in coding challenges.

scenario.title: Implementing a Fast Text Search Feature

skills.title: Geometric Algorithms
skills.why_this_skill: Geometric algorithms are used in graphics, robotics, and mapping applications, and can appear in advanced algorithmic interviews.

scenario.title: Detecting Overlapping Regions in a Map Application

skills.title: Foundations of System Design
skills.why_this_skill: Covers required domain: advanced_architecture

modules.title: System Design Case Studies
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module focuses on applying all learned system design principles to real-world case studies, preparing the learner for complex design interviews.

skills.title: Design YouTube/Netflix
skills.why_this_skill: Designing large-scale video streaming services tests knowledge of content delivery networks, distributed storage, encoding, and recommendation systems.

scenario.title: Optimizing Video Playback Latency Globally

skills.title: Design Twitter/Facebook Feed
skills.why_this_skill: Designing social media feeds involves complex challenges in fan-out, real-time updates, caching, and personalized content delivery.

scenario.title: Scaling a Real-time Notification System for Billions of Users

skills.title: Design a Distributed Cache
skills.why_this_skill: Designing a distributed cache involves understanding consistency, eviction policies, and fault tolerance in a critical performance component.

scenario.title: Implementing a Consistent Hashing Algorithm for Cache Distribution

skills.title: Core Concepts
skills.why_this_skill: Covers required domain: foundations

modules.title: Interview Simulation & Strategy
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills: This module focuses on the practical aspects of interview preparation, including mock interviews, behavioral questions, and resume optimization.

skills.title: Mock Coding Interviews
skills.why_this_skill: Simulating real coding interviews is crucial for practicing problem-solving under pressure, improving communication, and receiving targeted feedback.

scenario.title: Whiteboard Coding Challenge: Design a File System

skills.title: Behavioral Interview Preparation
skills.why_this_skill: Behavioral interviews assess soft skills, cultural fit, and past experiences, which are equally important as technical skills for landing a role.

scenario.title: Responding to 'Tell me about a time you failed'

skills.title: Caching & Performance Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: A compelling resume and portfolio are essential for securing interviews and showcasing your transformed capabilities to recruiters and hiring managers.

scenario.title: Reviewing and Enhancing a Peer's Resume for a Target Role

science.desc: Full mock interview combining a medium-hard coding problem, a system design question (e.g., 'Design a distributed rate limiter'), and behavioral questions.

customer_profile.current_identity: and my biggest struggle is the fear of not being able to crack the difficult interviews for product-based companies. My main goal is to switch to a Product-Based Company as a Software Engineer, aiming for better career growth. I know I really need to improve my Advanced DSA, System Design, and overall interview problem-solving skills to bridge these gaps. I feel moderately confident about my ability to achieve this, but I know it's a tough road ahead. I'm looking to make this career move within the next three months, and I can commit about 5 hours a week to my studies.
